key_points: The judiciary is an independent system of courts responsible for interpreting laws and delivering justice
It ensures that laws made by the legislature and actions of the government follow the Constitution
The judiciary protects Fundamental Rights of citizens and provides legal remedies when rights are violated
The Indian judicial system has a hierarchy consisting of the Supreme Court, High Courts and District Courts
The judiciary settles disputes between individuals, between citizens and government, and between states
Judicial review allows courts to examine laws and government actions to ensure they follow the Constitution
examples: A citizen can approach the Supreme Court if their Fundamental Rights are violated
High Courts hear appeals from lower courts within the state
District courts handle most civil and criminal cases at the local level
Courts can declare a law invalid if it goes against the Constitution
formulas: Judiciary → Interpretation of laws + Protection of rights
Court hierarchy → Supreme Court → High Court → District Court
Judicial Review → Court checks law + Constitution compatibility
exam_tips: Explain the meaning and role of the judiciary clearly
Remember the structure of the Indian court system
Mention how the judiciary protects Fundamental Rights
Use examples like judicial review or appeal system in answers
